summary: Iteration 3 testing complete. Backend (8/8 pytest passed) and Frontend (8/8 UI flows passed) both fully working. Verified live streak computation, /profile/onboard endpoint, PATCH /profile semantics, full onboarding gate with 3 steps, avatar tap -> /profile, account edit, and pattern edit-via-onboarding.

minor.issue: Hardcoded streak_days: 5 still returned here (unrelated to /api/profile.streak_days which is now correctly live-computed). Not in scope of iter3 but worth aligning.

action_items: Optional: align /api/wellness/scores.streak_days with the new compute_streak() helper so it matches /api/profile.streak_days.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py has duplicate code paths inside _seed_main() (the profile-insert + default-tasks block is repeated 3x at lines ~250-276). Functionally harmless because of the existence check, but should be deduped.

PATCH /api/profile silently drops streak_days (correct, since it is derived) — good. The contract is undocumented in the API response though; consider returning a 200 with a hint or simply leave as-is.

Onboarding.jsx onb-back button on step 0 calls onDone?() which in initial onboarding (no onDone wired for cancel) leaves the user on a still-onboarding-required state. Acceptable because Shell will re-show onboarding next render, but a subtle UX edge.

App.js: profile is fetched once in Shell. After edits inside /profile the streak in the global header could be stale until the next route mount. Header.jsx makes its own /profile call on mount, so it self-heals, but cross-component cache (Context) would be cleaner.
seed_data_creation: Test temporarily deletes /restores mood_entries for 'alex' to verify streak==0 case; original data is restored at the end of the test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iter3 requirements verified end-to-end: (1) GET /profile.streak_days is live (==7 with seed, ==0 after deleting moods, ignores client-supplied 999). (2) POST /profile/onboard sets onboarded=true, persists name, avatar_initial = first letter uppercased, persists your_pattern. (3) PATCH /profile updates name->avatar_initial and supports onboarded:false. (4) UI: onboarding screen full-screen when onboarded=false with bottom nav hidden; 3-step flow with proper enable/disable gating; avatar tap navigates to /profile; profile screen renders avatar/name/streak/income/pattern-grid; Edit account inputs work and persist; edit-pattern opens Onboarding in isEdit mode. After tests, demo user reset to {name:Alex, income:16000, onboarded:true} with a fully populated your_pattern.
